title: CRM for B2B SaaS Sales Teams

prd.oneLiner: A CRM that connects lead intake, accounts, opportunities, activities, tasks, and forecasting for B2B SaaS sales teams.
prd.goal: Within six months of launch, increase weekly follow-up coverage per seller by 30% and reduce stale pipeline records to less than 20%.
prd.whyNow: Small and mid-sized SaaS sales teams often outgrow spreadsheets before they can justify an enterprise CRM rollout.
prd.problem: Lead sources, account context, contact relationships, activity history, and next steps are scattered across spreadsheets, email, and project tools. Sellers lose context and miss follow-ups.
prd.solution: Use one account- and opportunity-centered data model to connect lead intake, pipeline management, accounts, contacts, activities, tasks, and sales reporting.
prd.alternatives: Teams combine spreadsheets, email labels, generic project-management tools, or a heavyweight enterprise CRM.
prd.differentiator: The default workflow is designed around the B2B SaaS journey from lead to account, opportunity, activity, next action, and forecast without requiring complex customization.

targets.name: Account Executive
targets.needs: Understand lead and account context quickly and never miss the next action.
targets.pain: Email, call notes, and tasks are fragmented, so follow-up timing is easy to miss.

targets.name: Sales Development Representative
targets.needs: Qualify new leads quickly and hand them to the right owner and pipeline stage.
targets.pain: Lead quality and duplicates are difficult to assess across different sources.

targets.name: Sales Manager
targets.needs: See pipeline health, activity, forecast, and bottlenecks across the team.
targets.pain: Stale opportunities and at-risk deals are discovered too late.

targets.name: Revenue Operations
targets.needs: Keep sales data quality, definitions, permissions, and process rules consistent.
targets.pain: Duplicate records and inconsistent stages make reporting unreliable.

scenarios.text: An operational user registers required work and checks its status.
An operator processes queued work and handles exceptions.
System and policy administrator reviews policy and access.
System and policy administrator reviews audit records and operating metrics.

prd.northStar: Share of active opportunities with a recorded next action

kpis.name: On-time processing rate
kpis.target: Within six months of launch, increase weekly follow-up coverage per seller by 30% and reduce stale pipeline records to less than 20%.
kpis.method: Monthly aggregation of state changes against target dates

kpis.name: Operations visibility
kpis.method: Share of records connected to reporting

inScope: Lead capture, duplicate detection, qualification, and assignment
Pipeline stages, opportunity amounts, probabilities, and close dates
Account and contact profiles with ownership and relationships
Call, email, meeting, and note activity logging
Tasks, due dates, reminders, and seller work queues
Pipeline and activity dashboards, automation, global search, and forecast analysis
Role-based access, team management, and configurable sales process settings
Lead inbox validation and duplicate prevention
nonGoals: A full marketing automation campaign builder
A replacement for a customer-support ticketing system
A complex CPQ pricing rules engine
assumptions: Initial customers are B2B SaaS sales organizations with 10 to 100 sellers.
Early lead and activity data arrives primarily through manual entry and CSV import.
Pipeline stages and required fields are managed at workspace level.
risks: Data quality and forecast accuracy decline if sellers do not record activities.
Migration from an existing CRM can be a substantial onboarding burden.
Too many mandatory fields can reduce adoption.
Broad email and calendar permissions can delay security review.
openQuestions: How much email and calendar synchronization should the first release support?
Should lead scoring begin as rules-based scoring or manual grading?
How much team, role, and regional permission configuration is required initially?
Which forecast categories and probability rules should be customizable by team?
constraints: The MVP is web-first; mobile web focuses on viewing and simple updates.
Personal and sales-sensitive data must use role-based access.
Core objects require auditable create and update history.
Permission, policy, and state changes require audit logs.
